name: Epic E-1 Foundation
description: Implement Epic E-1: Foundation & Remote State for the petclinic-platform infrastructure repo. This establishes the Terraform project structure, remote state backend (S3 + DynamoDB), provider configuration, and environment root modules for dev and prod in us-east-2.

goal.answer: Implement Epic E-1: Foundation & Remote State for the petclinic-platform infrastructure repo. This establishes the Terraform project structure, remote state backend (S3 + DynamoDB), provider configuration, and environment root modules for dev and prod in us-east-2.

criteria.answer:
terraform/environments/dev/ exists with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, backend.tf, terraform.tfvars
terraform/environments/prod/ exists with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, backend.tf, terraform.tfvars
terraform/modules/ exists with subdirectories vpc, eks, ecr, rds, dns, secrets, observability, each containing main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
scripts/bootstrap-state.sh provisions S3 state bucket and DynamoDB lock table idempotently with region default us-east-2
Both environment root modules have versions.tf pinning Terraform >= 1.6.0 and AWS provider ~> 5.0
Both environments have backend.tf pointing to S3 with keys petclinic/dev/terraform.tfstate and petclinic/prod/terraform.tfstate and DynamoDB locking
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev and terraform/environments/prod
.gitignore excludes sensitive files (*.tfvars, .env, *.pem, etc.) and allows .terraform.lock.hcl

constraints.answer: Use us-east-2 as the deployment region, No secrets committed to git, Follow project naming convention petclinic-{env}-{resource}, Use S3 + DynamoDB for remote state, Never store AWS credentials in code

phases.answer: Create directory structure and bootstrap script, Configure providers, backends, and root modules, Validate and verify

assumptions.answer: User has AWS CLI configured with credentials that can create S3 and DynamoDB resources; Terraform CLI >= 1.6.0 is installed; us-east-2 is the target region and t4g/Graviton free tier is available there; Bootstrap script is run manually once before terraform init

phases.name: Create directory structure and bootstrap script
phases.goal: Establish Terraform project layout and the state bootstrap script
phases.description: Create environment root module directories, module directories, placeholder files, and the bootstrap-state.sh script that idempotently creates the S3 bucket and DynamoDB table for Terraform remote state.

steps.description: Create terraform/environments/dev and prod directories with required files
Create terraform/modules subdirectories with placeholder main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf

verification.command: test -f terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf && test -f terraform/modules/eks/main.tf && test -f terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf && test -f terraform/modules/rds/main.tf && test -f terraform/modules/dns/main.tf && test -f terraform/modules/secrets/main.tf && test -f terraform/modules/observability/main.tf

steps.description: Create scripts/bootstrap-state.sh that provisions S3 + DynamoDB in us-east-2

phases.summary: Phase 1 completed: created Terraform environment root module directories (dev/prod) with required files, created seven module directories with placeholder files, and created scripts/bootstrap-state.sh for idempotent S3 + DynamoDB provisioning in us-east-2.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Configure providers, backends, and root modules
phases.goal: Wire up Terraform versions, AWS provider, S3 backend, and root module variables/outputs
phases.description: Add versions.tf with Terraform and AWS provider constraints, backend.tf with S3 + DynamoDB, providers.tf with default tags, and variables.tf/outputs.tf in both environment root modules.

steps.description: Add versions.tf in dev and prod with Terraform >= 1.6.0 and AWS provider ~> 5.0
Add backend.tf in dev and prod with correct S3 keys and DynamoDB lock table
Add providers.tf with AWS default tags Project, Environment, ManagedBy=terraform
Add variables.tf and outputs.tf in dev and prod root modules

verification.command: test -f terraform/environments/dev/variables.tf && test -f terraform/environments/dev/outputs.tf && test -f terraform/environments/prod/variables.tf && test -f terraform/environments/prod/outputs.tf

phases.summary: Configured Terraform versions, backends, providers, variables, and outputs for both dev and prod environment root modules. Created versions.tf with Terraform >= 1.6.0 and AWS provider ~> 5.0, backend.tf with S3 keys petclinic/dev/terraform.tfstate and petclinic/prod/terraform.tfstate plus DynamoDB locking, providers.tf with default tags, and variables.tf/outputs.tf with required declarations.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Validate and verify
phases.goal: Ensure Terraform configuration is syntactically valid and ready for init
phases.description: Run terraform fmt and terraform validate in both environment directories. Verify bootstrap script can be dry-run and that .gitignore is correctly configured.

steps.description: Run terraform validate in dev and prod

verification.command: cd terraform/environments/dev && terraform validate && cd ../prod && terraform validate

phases.summary: Validated Terraform configuration: ran terraform fmt -recursive (no changes needed), ran terraform init -backend=false and terraform validate in both dev and prod (both passed exit 0), and verified bootstrap-state.sh syntax and content (valid bash, us-east-2 default, S3+DynamoDB provisioning, LockID, public access block).

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

goal: Implement Epic E-1: Foundation & Remote State for the petclinic-platform infrastructure repo. This establishes the Terraform project structure, remote state backend (S3 + DynamoDB), provider configuration, and environment root modules for dev and prod in us-east-2.
successCriteria: terraform/environments/dev/ exists with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, backend.tf, terraform.tfvars
terraform/environments/prod/ exists with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, backend.tf, terraform.tfvars
terraform/modules/ exists with subdirectories vpc, eks, ecr, rds, dns, secrets, observability, each containing main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
scripts/bootstrap-state.sh provisions S3 state bucket and DynamoDB lock table idempotently with region default us-east-2
Both environment root modules have versions.tf pinning Terraform >= 1.6.0 and AWS provider ~> 5.0
Both environments have backend.tf pointing to S3 with keys petclinic/dev/terraform.tfstate and petclinic/prod/terraform.tfstate and DynamoDB locking
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev and terraform/environments/prod
.gitignore excludes sensitive files (*.tfvars, .env, *.pem, etc.) and allows .terraform.lock.hcl
constraints: Use us-east-2 as the deployment region
Use S3 + DynamoDB for remote state
Never store AWS credentials in code

grade.rationale: The visible diff is dominated by unrelated region-string changes in .claude files, and key success-criteria artifacts are not substantiated: terraform/environments/{dev,prod}/terraform.tfvars never appears in the file list or phase rationales, terraform/modules/ contents are hidden in the truncated '[... 29 more untracked files ...]' block, and .gitignore changes are not shown. Although phase gates claim real terraform validate and bootstrap-state.sh checks, the missing required files and lack of diff evidence for modules/terraform.tfvars make coverage suspect.
